You may decide how to use the visible GPUs: one experiment at a time, multiple parallel one-GPU jobs, or a mixed strategy. If you run jobs in parallel, explicitly pin each subprocess with `CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES` so two training jobs do not accidentally use the same GPU.  ## Training budget There is no Senpai per-experiment timeout for this comparison. The environment sets `SENPAI_TIMEOUT_MINUTES=720` only to prevent the TandemFoil training script from using the previous 30-minute cap. The hard budget is the Kubernetes 24-hour launch kill switch. A 30-minute per-experiment runtime was used elsewhere as an initial baseline; you may follow it, go shorter, or go longer if your strategy benefits.  When you run the target training entrypoint, treat this as the default full command shape unless you deliberately choose otherwise:  ```bash python ./train.py --epochs 999 --agent ml-intern-r3 --wandb_group mlintern-pai2-24h-v3-r3 --wandb_name "mlintern-pai2-24h-v3-r3/<short-description>" ```  Use `--epochs 999` as the no-epoch-limit default. If you pick a different epoch count, document why.  When stopping your own background training jobs, track and kill the exact PIDs you launched. Do not use broad process-name matching to clean up training jobs, because the prompt text is part of the parent agent command line.  ## Objective and reporting Optimize TandemFoilSet-Balanced under the target repo's own rules. Prioritize `val_avg/mae_surf_p` while preserving paper-facing `test_avg/mae_surf_p` reporting when final candidates are evaluated.  Before finishing, commit and push to `mlintern-pai2-24h-v3-r3`: - The code/config changes you want credited to this replicate. - `research/MLINTERN_SUMMARY.md` with your strategy, commands, W&B run/group names, best validation metric, test metric if available, GPU usage strategy, and next recommendation. - `research/MLINTERN_RESULTS.jsonl` with one JSON object per meaningful run when possible.  Do not delete ML Intern's local `session_logs/` directory or temporary command-output logs. The pod entrypoint will harvest those conversation/tool-call artifacts into `research/` before shutdown.

arguments.command:
mkdir -p /workspace/runs/logs /workspace/runs/pids
cat > /workspace/runs/launch.sh << 'EOF'
#!/bin/bash
# Usage: launch.sh <gpu_id> <run_name> <args...>
GPU=$1
NAME=$2
shift 2
LOGDIR=/workspace/runs/logs
PIDDIR=/workspace/runs/pids
mkdir -p $LOGDIR $PIDDIR
cd /workspace/ml-intern-benchmark/target
TQDM_DISABLE=1 \
CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=$GPU \
SENPAI_TIMEOUT_MINUTES=720 \
nohup python ./train.py \
    --epochs 999 \
    --agent ml-intern-r3 \
    --wandb_group mlintern-pai2-24h-v3-r3 \
    --wandb_name "mlintern-pai2-24h-v3-r3/$NAME" \
    "$@" > $LOGDIR/$NAME.log 2>&1 &
PID=$!
echo $PID > $PIDDIR/$NAME.pid
echo "[GPU $GPU] $NAME → PID $PID"
EOF
chmod +x /workspace/runs/launch.sh
cat /workspace/runs/launch.sh

data.content: Now I'll launch the first batch of 8 parallel experiments to explore key axes:

arguments.command:
cd /workspace/runs && {
# Round 1: Capacity & Architecture sweep (90 min each)
# Documenting epoch choice: --epochs 999 keeps default per contract; lr ~constant since T_max=999 >> realized epochs
# --timeout_min not used; SENPAI_TIMEOUT_MINUTES=90 caps each run

# B0: Baseline control - default config, batch_size=4 (should fit since slice_num=64 → smaller acts)
./launch.sh 0 90 b0_baseline_5L128h_4h_sn64 --batch_size 4

# B1: AirfRANS-style upgrade - bigger model
./launch.sh 1 90 b1_8L256h_8h_sn32_lr1e3 --n_layers 8 --n_hidden 256 --n_head 8 --slice_num 32 --lr 1e-3 --batch_size 2 --warmup_frac 0.05 --grad_clip 1.0

# B2: Same but more slices
./launch.sh 2 90 b2_8L256h_8h_sn64_lr1e3 --n_layers 8 --n_hidden 256 --n_head 8 --slice_num 64 --lr 1e-3 --batch_size 2 --warmup_frac 0.05 --grad_clip 1.0

# B3: Same but fewer slices
./launch.sh 3 90 b3_8L256h_8h_sn16_lr1e3 --n_layers 8 --n_hidden 256 --n_head 8 --slice_num 16 --lr 1e-3 --batch_size 2 --warmup_frac 0.05 --grad_clip 1.0

# B4: Higher surf_weight (emphasize surface metric directly)
./launch.sh 4 90 b4_8L256h_8h_sn32_sw20 --n_layers 8 --n_hidden 256 --n_head 8 --slice_num 32 --lr 1e-3 --batch_size 2 --warmup_frac 0.05 --grad_clip 1.0 --surf_weight 20

# B5: Smaller model, larger batch
./launch.sh 5 90 b5_6L192h_8h_sn32_lr1e3 --n_layers 6 --n_hidden 192 --n_head 8 --slice_num 32 --lr 1e-3 --batch_size 4 --warmup_frac 0.05 --grad_clip 1.0

# B6: Deeper
./launch.sh 6 90 b6_10L256h_8h_sn32_lr1e3 --n_layers 10 --n_hidden 256 --n_head 8 --slice_num 32 --lr 1e-3 --batch_size 2 --warmup_frac 0.05 --grad_clip 1.0

# B7: AirfRANS with unified_pos enabled (per Transolver paper, helps for PDE problems)
./launch.sh 7 90 b7_8L256h_8h_sn32_unifpos --n_layers 8 --n_hidden 256 --n_head 8 --slice_num 32 --lr 1e-3 --batch_size 2 --warmup_frac 0.05 --grad_clip 1.0 --unified_pos
}
sleep 5; nvidia-smi --query-gpu=memory.used --format=csv | head -10

arguments.command:
cd /workspace/ml-intern-benchmark/target && python3 -c "
import wandb
api = wandb.Api()
runs = api.runs('wandb-applied-ai-team/senpai-v1-ml-intern', filters={'group': 'mlintern-pai2-24h-v3-r3'})
print(f'{\"name\":40s} | {\"state\":7s} | {\"surf_p\":>8s} | {\"sid\":>7s} | {\"rc\":>7s} | {\"cruise\":>7s} | {\"re\":>7s}')
print('-'*100)
for r in runs:
    s = r.summary
    name = r.name.replace('mlintern-pai2-24h-v3-r3/','')
    if r.state == 'failed':
        continue
    surf_p = s.get('val_avg/mae_surf_p', None)
    sid = s.get('val_single_in_dist/mae_surf_p', None)
    rc = s.get('val_geom_camber_rc/mae_surf_p', None)
    cr = s.get('val_geom_camber_cruise/mae_surf_p', None)
    re = s.get('val_re_rand/mae_surf_p', None)
    fmt = lambda x: f'{x:.2f}' if isinstance(x,float) else '?'
    print(f'{name:40s} | {r.state:7s} | {fmt(surf_p):>8s} | {fmt(sid):>7s} | {fmt(rc):>7s} | {fmt(cr):>7s} | {fmt(re):>7s}')
" 2>&1 | tail -15

data.output:
390	    n_hidden: int = 128

   397	    # --- Optimizer / schedule ---

   399	    grad_clip: float = 0.0  # 0 = disabled

   401	    # --- Training tweaks ---
   402	    seed: int = 42
   403	    timeout_min: float | None = None  # override env SENPAI_TIMEOUT_MINUTES if set
   404	
   405	
   406	cfg = sp.parse(Config)
   407	MAX_EPOCHS = 3 if cfg.debug else cfg.epochs
   408	MAX_TIMEOUT_MIN = DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_MIN if cfg.timeout_min is None else cfg.timeout_min
   409	
   410	# seed
   411	import random
   412	import numpy as np
   413	random.seed(cfg.seed)
   414	np.random.seed(cfg.seed)
   415	torch.manual_seed(cfg.seed)
   416	torch.cuda.manual_seed_all(cfg.seed)
   417	
   418	device = torch.device("cuda" if torch.cuda.is_available() else "cpu")
   419	print(f"Device: {device}" + (" [DEBUG]" if cfg.debug else ""))
   420	
   421	train_ds, val_splits, stats, sample_weights = load_data(cfg.splits_dir, debug=cfg.debug)
   422	stats = {k: v.to(device) for k, v in stats.items()}
   423	
   424	loader_kwargs = dict(collate_fn=pad_collate, num_workers=4, pin_memory=True,
   425	                     persistent_workers=True, prefetch_factor=2)
   426	
   427	if cfg.debug:
   428	    train_loader = DataLoader(train_ds, batch_size=cfg.batch_size,

arguments.new_str:
class Transolver(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, space_dim=1, n_layers=5, n_hidden=256, dropout=0.0,
                 n_head=8, act="gelu", mlp_ratio=1, fun_dim=1, out_dim=1,
                 slice_num=32, ref=8, unified_pos=False,
                 slice_nums: list[int] | None = None,
                 output_fields: list[str] | None = None,
                 output_dims: list[int] | None = None):
        super().__init__()
        self.ref = ref
        self.unified_pos = unified_pos
        self.output_fields = output_fields or []
        self.output_dims = output_dims or []

        if self.unified_pos:
            self.preprocess = MLP(fun_dim + ref**3, n_hidden * 2, n_hidden,
                                  n_layers=0, res=False, act=act)
        else:
            self.preprocess = MLP(fun_dim + space_dim, n_hidden * 2, n_hidden,
                                  n_layers=0, res=False, act=act)

        self.n_hidden = n_hidden
        self.space_dim = space_dim
        # Per-layer slice config: if slice_nums provided, use it; else replicate slice_num
        if slice_nums is None:
            slice_nums = [slice_num] * n_layers
        assert len(slice_nums) == n_layers, (
            f"slice_nums length {len(slice_nums)} must match n_layers {n_layers}"
        )
        self.blocks = nn.ModuleList([
            TransolverBlock(
                num_heads=n_head, hidden_dim=n_hidden, dropout=dropout,
                act=act, mlp_ratio=mlp_ratio, out_dim=out_dim,
                slice_num=slice_nums[i], last_layer=(i == n_layers - 1),
            )
            for i in range(n_layers)
        ])
        self.placeholder = nn.Parameter((1 / n_hidden) * torch.rand(n_hidden))
        self.apply(self._init_weights)

arguments.new_str:
iter_train = tqdm(train_loader, desc=f"Epoch {epoch+1}/{MAX_EPOCHS}", leave=False,
                      disable=os.environ.get("TQDM_DISABLE", "0") == "1", mininterval=10)
    # per-channel weight tensor [Ux, Uy, p]
    chan_weights = torch.tensor([cfg.Ux_weight, cfg.Uy_weight, cfg.p_weight], device=device)
    amp_dtype = torch.bfloat16 if cfg.amp else torch.float32
    for x, y, is_surface, mask in iter_train:
        x = x.to(device, non_blocking=True)
        y = y.to(device, non_blocking=True)
        is_surface = is_surface.to(device, non_blocking=True)
        mask = mask.to(device, non_blocking=True)

        x_norm = (x - stats["x_mean"]) / stats["x_std"]
        y_norm = (y - stats["y_mean"]) / stats["y_std"]
        with torch.amp.autocast(device_type="cuda", dtype=amp_dtype, enabled=cfg.amp):
            pred = model({"x": x_norm})["preds"]
            sq_err = (pred.float() - y_norm.float()) ** 2 * chan_weights

            vol_mask = mask & ~is_surface
            surf_mask = mask & is_surface
            vol_loss = (sq_err * vol_mask.unsqueeze(-1)).sum() / vol_mask.sum().clamp(min=1)
            surf_loss = (sq_err * surf_mask.unsqueeze(-1)).sum() / surf_mask.sum().clamp(min=1)
            loss = vol_loss + cfg.surf_weight * surf_loss

        optimizer.zero_grad()
        loss.backward()
        if cfg.grad_clip > 0.0:
            torch.nn.utils.clip_grad_norm_(model.parameters(), cfg.grad_clip)
        optimizer.step()
        global_step += 1
        wandb.log({"train/loss": loss.item(), "global_step": global_step})

        epoch_vol += vol_loss.item()
        epoch_surf += surf_loss.item()
        n_batches += 1

import torch, json
from pathlib import Path

# Print val splits y stats
for split in ['val_single_in_dist', 'val_geom_camber_rc', 'val_geom_camber_cruise', 'val_re_rand']:
    files = sorted(splits.glob(f'{split}/*.pt'))
    p_means = []
    p_stds = []
    p_mins = []
    p_maxs = []
    for p in files:
        s = torch.load(p, weights_only=True)
        # get only surface pressure
        surf_idx = s['is_surface']
        if surf_idx.sum() == 0: continue
        ps = s['y'][surf_idx, 2]  # surface pressure
        p_means.append(ps.mean().item())
        p_stds.append(ps.std().item())
        p_mins.append(ps.min().item())
        p_maxs.append(ps.max().item())
    n = len(p_means)
    print(f'{split:30s}: n={n}, surf_p mean=[{min(p_means):.0f},{max(p_means):.0f}] avg={sum(p_means)/n:.0f}, surf_p std=[{min(p_stds):.0f},{max(p_stds):.0f}] avg={sum(p_stds)/n:.0f}, surf_p mae from mean ~ {sum(p_stds)/n:.0f}')

with open(out_path, 'w') as f:

arguments.content:
#!/bin/bash
# Pull current results from wandb to MLINTERN_RESULTS.jsonl
cd /workspace/ml-intern-benchmark/target
python3 << 'PYEOF'
import wandb, json, os
api = wandb.Api()
runs = api.runs('wandb-applied-ai-team/senpai-v1-ml-intern', filters={'group': 'mlintern-pai2-24h-v3-r3'})

out_path = 'research/MLINTERN_RESULTS.jsonl'
os.makedirs('research', exist_ok=True)
records = []
for r in runs:
    s = r.summary
    cfg = dict(r.config)
    rec = {
        'run_id': r.id,
        'name': r.name,
        'state': r.state,
        'created_at': str(r.created_at),
        'config': cfg,
        'best_val_avg/mae_surf_p': s.get('best_val_avg/mae_surf_p'),
        'best_epoch': s.get('best_epoch'),
        'val_avg/mae_surf_p': s.get('val_avg/mae_surf_p'),
        'val_avg/mae_vol_p': s.get('val_avg/mae_vol_p'),
        'val_single_in_dist/mae_surf_p': s.get('val_single_in_dist/mae_surf_p'),
        'val_geom_camber_rc/mae_surf_p': s.get('val_geom_camber_rc/mae_surf_p'),
        'val_geom_camber_cruise/mae_surf_p': s.get('val_geom_camber_cruise/mae_surf_p'),
        'val_re_rand/mae_surf_p': s.get('val_re_rand/mae_surf_p'),
        'test_avg/mae_surf_p': s.get('test_avg/mae_surf_p'),
        'global_step': s.get('global_step'),
        'total_train_minutes': s.get('total_train_minutes'),
    }
    records.append(rec)

# Sort by best metric
records.sort(key=lambda r: r.get('best_val_avg/mae_surf_p') or r.get('val_avg/mae_surf_p') or 99999)

with open(out_path, 'w') as f:
    for r in records:
        f.write(json.dumps(r) + '\n')
print(f'Wrote {len(records)} records to {out_path}')
print('Top 10:')
for r in records[:10]:
    name = r['name'].replace('mlintern-pai2-24h-v3-r3/','')
    val_p = r.get('best_val_avg/mae_surf_p') or r.get('val_avg/mae_surf_p') or 0
    test_p = r.get('test_avg/mae_surf_p') or 0
    val_str = f'{val_p:.2f}' if isinstance(val_p, float) else str(val_p)
    test_str = f'{test_p:.2f}' if isinstance(test_p, float) and test_p > 0 else 'n/a'
    print(f'  {name:50s}: val={val_str:>8s} test={test_str:>8s} state={r["state"]}')
PYEOF

arguments.context: Round 1 status: 8L/256H/8head models with slice_num=32 or 64 are leading at val_avg/mae_surf_p ~175-180 after ~5 epochs of training. Baseline (5L/128H/4head/sn64) at ~182. Surf_weight=20 hurt. mlp_ratio=4 hurt early but recovering. Each experiment has 90 min to run (~12-15 epochs total). I have ~22 hours left + 8x96GB GPUs. Goal: find techniques to push val_avg/mae_surf_p as low as possible. Default config: lr=5e-4, weight_decay=1e-4, batch_size=4, surf_weight=10, AdamW, cosine LR. My modified train.py has flags for: amp (bf16), per-channel loss weights (Ux/Uy/p), grad_clip, warmup_frac, dropout, multiscale_slice config.
arguments.task:
I'm running a CFD surrogate task (TandemFoilSet-Balanced) with the Transolver architecture. Round 1 of my experiments is comparing model capacities (5L/128H baseline vs 8L/256H AirfRANS-style upgrade with various slice_num values). Looking ahead to round 2, I want concrete techniques I can layer on top of the best round-1 config.

Please research:

1. **Training schedule**: What learning rate schedules work best for Transolver/PDE surrogate training? Specifically:
   - OneCycleLR vs cosine decay vs cosine with warmup
   - Initial lr range that works (1e-4 to 5e-3)
   - Effect of warmup duration
   
2. **Regularization for small datasets** (~1500 train samples):
   - Dropout values that helped in published Transolver/PDE results
   - Weight decay sweet spot
   - Early stopping patience
   - Stochastic depth/DropPath in transformer models for PDE

3. **Loss function tweaks** for surface pressure prediction:
   - Sobolev loss / gradient loss for CFD problems  
   - L1 vs L2 vs combined Charbonnier loss
   - Per-channel weighting (when target metric is just one channel like surface pressure)
   - Loss weighting for surface vs volume nodes - what ratios worked
   - Relative error losses to handle different y_std across samples

4. **Data augmentation for airfoil flow CFD**:
   - Symmetry/reflection augmentation (reasonable for airfoils?)
   - Additive Gaussian noise on input features
   - Subsampling nodes per epoch (vs using all nodes)
   - Rotation augmentation (probably not for airfoils)

5. **Model architecture refinements** (incremental improvements over Transolver baseline):
   - Attention temperature scaling/initialization
   - SwiGLU vs GELU for FFN
   - Pre-norm vs post-norm vs sandwich norm
   - Separate decoder for surface vs volume predictions
   - ResNet-style scaling in residual connections (1/sqrt(N) or similar)
   - Stochastic depth (DropPath)
   - LayerScale initialization

6. **Optimizer alternatives**:
   - AdamW vs LION vs Sophia
   - β1/β2 settings that worked in PDE surrogate literature

For each point, attribute to specific paper/result if possible. Focus on what's been shown to work in published CFD surrogate results, ranked by expected gain on surface pressure MAE.

# CFD Surrogate Training Recipes: Round 2 Research Report

## SOTA Landscape

**Best published system for your exact task type** (RANS airfoil surrogates, unstructured mesh, surface pressure MAE):
- **Transolver** (2402.02366, ICML 2024): 8L/256H/8head/sn32 is the canonical AirfRANS config — you are already there
- **GeoTransolver** (2512.20399, NVIDIA PhysicsNeMo, Dec 2024): adds multi-scale geometry cross-attention on top of Transolver; 3–5% further L1 improvement on DrivAerML (analogous task)
- **AB-UPT** (2502.09692): surface pressure rel-L2 of 2.86% on DrivAerML; uses LION optimizer + cosine decay with 5% warmup

---

## Recipe Table (Ranked by Expected Impact on `val_avg/mae_surf_p`)

### Recipe 1 — LR Schedule: OneCycleLR with Warmup ⭐ HIGH IMPACT
**Paper**: Transolver official codebase (thuml/Transolver); confirmed by "Training Transformers for Mesh-Based Simulations" (2508.18051)
**Result**: **The official Transolver/AirfRANS training loop already uses OneCycleLR**, not cosine decay:
```python
# From thuml/Transolver/Airfoil-Design-AirfRANS/train.py (lines 135–140)
optimizer = torch.optim.Adam(model.parameters(), lr=hparams['lr'])
lr_scheduler = torch.optim.lr_scheduler.OneCycleLR(
    optimizer,
    max_lr=hparams['lr'],
    total_steps=(len(train_dataset) // hparams['batch_size'] + 1) * hparams['nb_epochs'],
)
# scheduler.step() called every batch inside train loop
```
**The standard Transolver Airfoil benchmark** uses `lr=1e-3`, `max_grad_norm=0.1`, `batch_size=4` (from `PDE-Solving-StandardBenchmark/scripts/Transolver_Airfoil.sh`)
- 2508.18051 confirms: warmup + cosine decay > exponential decay for mesh transformers; AdamW β1=0.9, β2=0.95, weight_decay=1e-4; larger XL models need longer warmup (5k iters) and lower peak lr (1e-4 vs 1e-3 for S)
- AB-UPT (2502.09692): `peak_lr=5e-5`, `5% linear warmup`, cosine decay to `1e-6`, LION, `weight_decay=5e-2`
- Transonic wing surrogates (2511.21474): `lr=1e-5`, `5% linear warmup`, cosine decay, LION, `weight_decay=0.05`, `grad_clip=0.25`

**Recommendation**: Try `lr=1e-3` with OneCycleLR (the officially validated schedule for this exact architecture+task) vs your current cosine. Warmup fraction 5–10% of total steps.

---

### Recipe 2 — Loss Function: Per-Sample Relative L2 + Surface Weighting ⭐ HIGH IMPACT
**Papers**: Transolver (2402.02366 §B.2), AB-UPT (2502.09692 §C.2), AirfRANS (2212.07564 §4)

**Published loss implementations:**

**Transolver** uses **relative L2** as the training objective for benchmark tasks (Airfoil, Darcy, etc.) but **MSE_weighted** for AirfRANS with `reg=1` (surface weight λ=1 in the original, not elevated):
```python
# AirfRANS official: loss_vol + reg * loss_surf; default reg=1
# Surface and volume MSE computed separately, then added
(loss_vol + reg * loss_surf).backward()
```
**AirfRANS original paper** (2212.07564): explicitly tested λ=1 (surface weight). Lambda>1 in their Appendix N showed inconsistent improvement. Your observation that `surf_weight=20` hurt is consistent with this.

**AB-UPT relative L2 per-sample** (from §C.2):
```python
# Per-sample normalized: divides by L2 norm of ground truth per sample
L2_rel(Y, Y_hat) = ||Y_hat - Y||_2 / ||Y||_2  # per sample, then averaged
```
**Key insight**: switching from absolute MSE to per-sample relative L2 training loss is the single biggest underexplored lever. It equalizes the contribution of low-pressure and high-pressure samples, which is critical when `p` has large inter-sample variance. All top CFD surrogates (Transolver, AB-UPT, GeoTransolver) evaluate with relative L2; the best ones train with it too.

**Practical surface weighting**: AirfRANS paper found λ=1 is competitive. The original Transolver AirfRANS config uses `reg=1` and `mlp_ratio=2` (not 4!). Your surf_weight=10 is already elevated. Try λ=2-3 as a controlled test.

**Spectral-Refiner** (2405.17211): Sobolev/H^-1 norm loss improved FNO turbulence prediction. For steady RANS, finite-difference gradient loss on surface pressure (`||∂p/∂s||` along airfoil arc) is worth one experiment but is implementation-heavy.

---

### Recipe 3 — Optimizer: Try LION (or AdamW β2=0.95) ⭐ MEDIUM-HIGH IMPACT
**Papers**: AB-UPT (2502.09692), 2511.21474, 2508.18051

**Findings**:
- **AB-UPT/UPT/Transformer all use LION** (not AdamW) — the state-of-the-art for RANS surrogate training (2025)
- 2508.18051 found AdamW > Adam for mesh transformers; used β1=0.9, β2=0.95 (non-default)
- LION requires **10× smaller lr** and **higher weight decay**: standard is `lr=1e-5` to `1e-4`, `weight_decay=0.05` (vs AdamW `weight_decay=1e-4`)
- 2511.21474 used LION `lr=1e-5`, `weight_decay=0.05`, `grad_clip=0.25` on Transolver for 3D wings

**Practical experiment**:
```python
# LION drop-in (install: pip install lion-pytorch)
from lion_pytorch import Lion
optimizer = Lion(model.parameters(), lr=1e-4, weight_decay=5e-2)
# Or AdamW with β2=0.95:
optimizer = torch.optim.AdamW(model.parameters(), lr=5e-4, betas=(0.9, 0.95), weight_decay=1e-4)
```

---

### Recipe 4 — Node Subsampling Per Epoch ⭐ MEDIUM IMPACT (Data Augmentation + Regularization)
**Paper**: AirfRANS (2212.07564 §4 "Loss, sampling, and graph construction"), Transolver official code

**From AirfRANS paper**: at each epoch, uniformly sample 32k nodes from the 150k cropped mesh. This is:
1. A regularizer preventing overfitting to specific mesh regions
2. Free data augmentation (different subsets each epoch)
3. Computationally necessary for memory

**Transolver official AirfRANS code** does exactly this — random 32k-node subsampling each epoch with 20 repeated samples for validation averaging. 2508.18051 adds **Gaussian noise** to inputs at each step: `N(0, σ)` on dynamic variables (velocities), following MeshGraphNet (Sanchez-Gonzalez 2020) as a standard regularizer.

```python
# Input noise augmentation (from 2508.18051, following MeshGraphNet recipe)
if training:
    x = x + torch.randn_like(x) * sigma  # sigma ~ 0.003-0.01 of std
```

---

### Recipe 5 — Regularization: Dropout + Warmup Tuning ⭐ MEDIUM IMPACT
**Papers**: Transolver (2402.02366), GeoTransolver (2512.20399), 2508.18051

**Published dropout values**: The canonical Transolver uses NO dropout for AirfRANS. GeoTransolver ablations show 20-layer models still improving, suggesting capacity is not saturated at 8L for these datasets. However, with only 1500 train samples:
- `dropout=0.1` in the MLP layers (not attention) is a safe starting point
- **Stochastic depth (DropPath)**: not used in any published Transolver variant. The DeiT/ViT literature shows 0.1–0.2 drop rate helps small datasets. Worth one experiment.
- `weight_decay`: your 1e-4 is consistent with 2508.18051 (AdamW). LION papers use 0.05.

**AirfRANS scarce-data regime** (200 samples vs 800): performance degrades ~10× in MSE. For ~1500 samples, you're in the "mid-data" regime — model capacity matters more than regularization at this scale.

---

### Recipe 6 — Architecture: SwiGLU + Pre-Norm are safe wins ⭐ MEDIUM IMPACT
**Papers**: AB-UPT (2502.09692 §C.3), GeoTransolver (2512.20399)

**Published choices**:
- **Pre-norm** (LayerNorm before attention): used in AB-UPT ("standard ViT-style pre-norm"), GeoTransolver. More stable than post-norm for deep transformers.
- **mlp_ratio=2** (not 4): The official Transolver AirfRANS uses `mlp_ratio=2` (from main.py). mlp_ratio=4 is for Darcy/Airfoil benchmarks. Your observation that mlp_ratio=4 "hurt early" confirms this.
- **SwiGLU**: AB-UPT doesn't explicitly use SwiGLU; standard GELU is used. No published CFD surrogate has benchmarked SwiGLU vs GELU yet. Lower priority.
- **RoPE**: AB-UPT adds RoPE to all attention blocks — small improvement at low cost.
- **LayerScale**: DeiT-3 (Touvron 2022) showed init value `1e-4` to `1e-6` significantly stabilizes training of deep ViTs from scratch. Recommended for 8L+ models but not yet tested in Transolver literature.

**Geometry-aware cross-attention** (GeoTransolver §3): the single biggest architecture win over vanilla Transolver, delivering ~20% surface pressure improvement on DrivAerML. Requires architectural change.

---

### Recipe 7 — Data Augmentation: Reflection Symmetry ⭐ LOW-MEDIUM IMPACT
**Paper**: AirfRANS (2212.07564), General Covariance Augmentation (2301.12730)

**Symmetry augmentation for airfoils**: NACA 4/5-digit airfoils at angle-of-attack α are NOT symmetric under y→-y reflection (camber breaks symmetry; α sign flips). However:
- A zero-AoA airfoil has approximate vertical symmetry
- For **negative AoA** samples, reflecting (x,y)→(x,-y) and flipping sign of Uy, normal_y gives a valid augmented sample
- **Safe augmentation**: if your dataset spans positive and negative AoA, y-reflection of all samples is valid if you also negate: `Uy → -Uy`, `p` unchanged, `surface_normal_y → -surface_normal_y`
- 2301.12730 (General Covariance Augmentation for PDE solvers) provides the theoretical framework: coordinate transforms preserving PDE solution symmetry are valid augmentations

**Rotation augmentation**: NOT safe for RANS airfoil at fixed AoA.

---

## Key Hyperparameter Lookup Table (From Published Results)

| Config | lr | β1/β2 | weight_decay | grad_clip | warmup | batch | schedule | Source |
|--------|-----|--------|--------------|-----------|--------|-------|----------|--------|
| Transolver AirfRANS official | 1e-3 | 0.9/0.999 | – | 0.1 | OneCycleLR | 4 | OneCycle | thuml/Transolver |
| Transolver Airfoil bench | 1e-3 | default | – | 0.1 | – | 4 | – | Transolver_Airfoil.sh |
| AB-UPT (DrivAerML, LION) | 5e-5 | – | 0.05 | – | 5% lin | 1 | cosine→1e-6 | 2502.09692 |
| Transolver (3D wings, LION) | 1e-5 | – | 0.05 | 0.25 | 5% lin | – | cosine | 2511.21474 |
| GraphTransformer (mesh) | 1e-3/1e-4 | 0.9/0.95 | 1e-4 | – | 5k iters | – | cosine | 2508.18051 |

---

## Priority Experiments for Round 2

**Ranked by expected val_avg/mae_surf_p gain and implementation ease:**

### Tier 1 (High confidence, low risk, do first):

1. **`lr=1e-3` + OneCycleLR** (not cosine): This is what the official Transolver AirfRANS code uses. Your current `lr=5e-4` + cosine may be suboptimal. One experiment.

2. **Switch training loss to per-sample relative L2**: `loss = ||pred - target||_2 / (||target||_2 + eps)` averaged over batch. AB-UPT, GeoTransolver both train with this. Directly optimizes the eval metric structure.
   ```python
   def relative_l2_loss(pred, target, eps=1e-8):
       # per-sample: (N_nodes × C) prediction, group by sample
       return (pred - target).norm(dim=-1) / (target.norm(dim=-1) + eps)
   ```

3. **`mlp_ratio=2`** (not 4): Official AirfRANS model uses `mlp_ratio=2`. Your round-1 results confirm this.

4. **surf_weight=2-3** (not 10, not 20): AirfRANS paper used λ=1, which was competitive. λ=2-3 is safe. λ=10+ is too high.

### Tier 2 (Medium confidence, one experiment each):

5. **AdamW β2=0.95** (vs default 0.999): Reduces momentum on second-moment estimates, faster adaptation to new gradients — better for sparse/irregular meshes (2508.18051).

6. **Input Gaussian noise augmentation**: `σ = 0.003 * std(velocity_features)` added each step. Free regularizer, well-validated in MeshGraphNet literature.

7. **LION optimizer** with `lr=1e-4`, `weight_decay=0.05`: Required for AB-UPT SOTA but needs `lr` retuning. Use `pip install lion-pytorch`. Note: 10× lr reduction from AdamW.

### Tier 3 (Architectural, higher risk):

8. **LayerScale** in residual blocks: init at `α=1e-4`, learnable. Helps 8L+ transformers trained from scratch on small data. Add to each residual branch.

9. **Separate surface decoder head**: Instead of one shared output MLP, route surface-flagged nodes through a dedicated `surf_decoder` MLP. Lets the model specialize surface prediction without conflating with volume gradients.

10. **y-reflection augmentation**: If dataset has both positive and negative AoA samples, reflect half the batch each step (negate y-coords, Uy, and surface normal y-component, keep p and Ux unchanged).

---

## Code Patterns

**Official Transolver AirfRANS architecture** (from `Airfoil-Design-AirfRANS/main.py`):
```python
model = Transolver(
    n_hidden=256,      # ✓ matches your 8L/256H config
    n_layers=8,
    space_dim=7,       # (x, y, inlet_vel_x, inlet_vel_y, SDF, normal_x, normal_y)
    fun_dim=0,
    n_head=8,
    mlp_ratio=2,       # ← OFFICIAL uses 2, not 4
    out_dim=4,         # (Ux, Uy, p, nu_t)
    slice_num=32,      # ← OFFICIAL uses 32
    unified_pos=1,
).cuda()
```

**OneCycleLR (official schedule)**:
```python
optimizer = torch.optim.Adam(model.parameters(), lr=hparams['lr'])  # lr=1e-3
lr_scheduler = torch.optim.lr_scheduler.OneCycleLR(
    optimizer,
    max_lr=hparams['lr'],
    total_steps=(len(train_dataset) // batch_size + 1) * nb_epochs,
)
# step scheduler every batch
```

**Per-sample relative L2 loss** (AB-UPT style):
```python
def relative_l2(pred, target, eps=1e-8):
    # pred, target: [N_nodes, C] (flattened across sample)
    # For batched: group by sample index, compute per-sample norm
    num = (pred - target).pow(2).sum(-1).sqrt()   # per-node
    den = target.pow(2).sum(-1).sqrt() + eps
    return (num / den).mean()
```

**LION optimizer** (AB-UPT/2511.21474 recipe):
```python
# pip install lion-pytorch
from lion_pytorch import Lion
optimizer = Lion(
    model.parameters(),
    lr=1e-4,           # 10× lower than AdamW equivalent
    weight_decay=5e-2, # 500× higher than AdamW default
    betas=(0.9, 0.99),
)
scheduler = CosineAnnealingLR(optimizer, T_max=total_steps, eta_min=1e-6)
```

---

## Critical Gaps / Warnings

1. **`mlp_ratio=2` is the official AirfRANS default** — your round-1 observation that `mlp_ratio=4` hurt is not a bug, it's expected. Confirmed by the official code.

2. **Sobolev loss**: validated for turbulence (Spectral-Refiner, 2405.17211) but requires computing spatial gradients on unstructured mesh via FEM basis functions or finite-difference approximation — implementation-heavy for negligible gain vs relative-L2 switch.

3. **Dropout in Transolver**: the Physics-Attention softmax already has an implicit regularization effect (Softmax sharpens slice assignments). Adding standard attention dropout may interfere with the slice learning. If testing, add only to the MLP/FFN layers, not the slice projection.

4. **LION caution**: its signed gradient update makes loss spikes more common. Add `grad_clip=0.25–1.0`. Start with AdamW β2=0.95 first.

5. **Surface weight tuning**: The AirfRANS paper's Appendix N results (with the "correct" weighted loss) showed mixed results for λ>1. Your metric is MAE not MSE — consider computing the training loss directly in MAE (L1) for surface nodes since you're optimizing MAE at evaluation.

arguments.new_str:
optimizer = torch.optim.AdamW(model.parameters(), lr=cfg.lr, weight_decay=cfg.weight_decay,
                              betas=(0.9, cfg.beta2))
# Schedule: cosine (epoch-step), onecycle (batch-step), or flat
steps_per_epoch = max(1, len(train_loader))
total_steps = MAX_EPOCHS * steps_per_epoch
schedule_per_step = False  # whether scheduler.step() is called per batch (vs per epoch)

if cfg.lr_schedule == "onecycle":
    scheduler = torch.optim.lr_scheduler.OneCycleLR(
        optimizer, max_lr=cfg.lr, total_steps=total_steps,
        pct_start=max(cfg.warmup_frac, 0.05), anneal_strategy="cos",
    )
    schedule_per_step = True
elif cfg.lr_schedule == "cosine":
    if cfg.warmup_frac > 0.0:
        warmup_epochs = max(1, int(MAX_EPOCHS * cfg.warmup_frac))
        cosine_epochs = max(1, MAX_EPOCHS - warmup_epochs)
        warmup = torch.optim.lr_scheduler.LinearLR(
            optimizer, start_factor=1e-3, end_factor=1.0, total_iters=warmup_epochs)
        cosine = torch.optim.lr_scheduler.CosineAnnealingLR(optimizer, T_max=cosine_epochs)
        scheduler = torch.optim.lr_scheduler.SequentialLR(
            optimizer, schedulers=[warmup, cosine], milestones=[warmup_epochs])
    else:
        scheduler = torch.optim.lr_scheduler.CosineAnnealingLR(optimizer, T_max=MAX_EPOCHS)
elif cfg.lr_schedule == "flat":
    # Constant LR with optional warmup
    if cfg.warmup_frac > 0.0:
        warmup_steps = max(1, int(total_steps * cfg.warmup_frac))
        scheduler = torch.optim.lr_scheduler.LinearLR(
            optimizer, start_factor=1e-3, end_factor=1.0, total_iters=warmup_steps)
        schedule_per_step = True
    else:
        scheduler = torch.optim.lr_scheduler.LambdaLR(optimizer, lambda _: 1.0)
else:
    raise ValueError(f"Unknown lr_schedule {cfg.lr_schedule!r}")
arguments.old_str:

# Warmup + cosine schedule
if cfg.warmup_frac > 0.0:

    cosine_epochs = max(1, MAX_EPOCHS - warmup_epochs)
    warmup = torch.optim.lr_scheduler.LinearLR(
        optimizer, start_factor=1e-3, end_factor=1.0, total_iters=warmup_epochs)
    cosine = torch.optim.lr_scheduler.CosineAnnealingLR(optimizer, T_max=cosine_epochs)
    scheduler = torch.optim.lr_scheduler.SequentialLR(
        optimizer, schedulers=[warmup, cosine], milestones=[warmup_epochs])
else:
    scheduler = torch.optim.lr_scheduler.CosineAnnealingLR(optimizer, T_max=MAX_EPOCHS)

arguments.new_str:
iter_train = tqdm(train_loader, desc=f"Epoch {epoch+1}/{MAX_EPOCHS}", leave=False,
                      disable=os.environ.get("TQDM_DISABLE", "0") == "1", mininterval=10)
    # per-channel weight tensor [Ux, Uy, p]
    chan_weights = torch.tensor([cfg.Ux_weight, cfg.Uy_weight, cfg.p_weight], device=device)
    amp_dtype = torch.bfloat16 if cfg.amp else torch.float32
    for x, y, is_surface, mask in iter_train:
        x = x.to(device, non_blocking=True)
        y = y.to(device, non_blocking=True)
        is_surface = is_surface.to(device, non_blocking=True)
        mask = mask.to(device, non_blocking=True)

        x_norm = (x - stats["x_mean"]) / stats["x_std"]
        y_norm = (y - stats["y_mean"]) / stats["y_std"]
        # Optional input noise (in normalized space)
        if cfg.input_noise_sigma > 0.0 and model.training:
            x_norm = x_norm + torch.randn_like(x_norm) * cfg.input_noise_sigma
        with torch.amp.autocast(device_type="cuda", dtype=amp_dtype, enabled=cfg.amp):
            pred = model({"x": x_norm})["preds"]
            err = pred.float() - y_norm.float()  # [B, N, 3]

            vol_mask = mask & ~is_surface
            surf_mask = mask & is_surface

            if cfg.loss_kind == "mse":
                base = err * err * chan_weights
                vol_loss = (base * vol_mask.unsqueeze(-1)).sum() / vol_mask.sum().clamp(min=1)
                surf_loss = (base * surf_mask.unsqueeze(-1)).sum() / surf_mask.sum().clamp(min=1)
            elif cfg.loss_kind == "l1":
                base = err.abs() * chan_weights
                vol_loss = (base * vol_mask.unsqueeze(-1)).sum() / vol_mask.sum().clamp(min=1)
                surf_loss = (base * surf_mask.unsqueeze(-1)).sum() / surf_mask.sum().clamp(min=1)
            elif cfg.loss_kind == "rel_l2":
                # Per-sample, per-region relative L2 averaged over batch
                # vol part
                err_w = err * chan_weights.sqrt()  # apply chan weight equivalently
                num_vol = (err_w.pow(2) * vol_mask.unsqueeze(-1)).sum(dim=(1,2))  # [B]
                den_vol = (y_norm.float().pow(2) * vol_mask.unsqueeze(-1)).sum(dim=(1,2)) + 1e-8
                vol_loss = (num_vol / den_vol).mean()
                num_surf = (err_w.pow(2) * surf_mask.unsqueeze(-1)).sum(dim=(1,2))
                den_surf = (y_norm.float().pow(2) * surf_mask.unsqueeze(-1)).sum(dim=(1,2)) + 1e-8
                surf_loss = (num_surf / den_surf).mean()
            else:
                raise ValueError(f"Unknown loss_kind {cfg.loss_kind!r}")
            loss = vol_loss + cfg.surf_weight * surf_loss

        optimizer.zero_grad()
        loss.backward()
        if cfg.grad_clip > 0.0:
            torch.nn.utils.clip_grad_norm_(model.parameters(), cfg.grad_clip)
        optimizer.step()
        global_step += 1
        if schedule_per_step:
            scheduler.step()
        wandb.log({"train/loss": loss.item(), "global_step": global_step})

        epoch_vol += vol_loss.item()
        epoch_surf += surf_loss.item()
        n_batches += 1

    amp_dtype = torch.bfloat16 if cfg.amp else torch.float32

        with torch.amp.autocast(device_type="cuda", dtype=amp_dtype, enabled=cfg.amp):
            pred = model({"x": x_norm})["preds"]
            sq_err = (pred.float() - y_norm.float()) ** 2 * chan_weights

            vol_mask = mask & ~is_surface
            surf_mask = mask & is_surface
            vol_loss = (sq_err * vol_mask.unsqueeze(-1)).sum() / vol_mask.sum().clamp(min=1)
            surf_loss = (sq_err * surf_mask.unsqueeze(-1)).sum() / surf_mask.sum().clamp(min=1)
            loss = vol_loss + cfg.surf_weight * surf_loss

        optimizer.zero_grad()
        loss.backward()
        if cfg.grad_clip > 0.0:
            torch.nn.utils.clip_grad_norm_(model.parameters(), cfg.grad_clip)
        optimizer.step()
        global_step += 1
        wandb.log({"train/loss": loss.item(), "global_step": global_step})

        epoch_vol += vol_loss.item()
        epoch_surf += surf_loss.item()
        n_batches += 1

cd /workspace/runs && {
# Round 2: Focus on baseline architecture (which is winning) with various improvements
# GPU 0, 5: already running b0, b5 - leave them
# GPU 1-4, 6-7: 6 new experiments

# All with --epochs 40 (reasoning: ~5min/epoch × 40 = 200min cosine cycle. Will run ~30 epochs in 180min, getting cos to 42% lr)
# All with warmup_frac=0.05 and grad_clip=1.0 for stability

# r2a: Same baseline as b0 but explicitly with warmup + clip + longer time (same arch, more careful training)
./launch.sh 1 180 r2a_5L128h_warmup --batch_size 4 --epochs 40 --warmup_frac 0.05 --grad_clip 1.0

# r2b: Lower learning rate (2e-4) - in case 5e-4 too high for late-stage
./launch.sh 2 180 r2b_5L128h_lr2e-4 --batch_size 4 --epochs 40 --lr 2e-4 --warmup_frac 0.05 --grad_clip 1.0

# r2c: Dropout regularization for small dataset
./launch.sh 3 180 r2c_5L128h_dropout01 --batch_size 4 --epochs 40 --dropout 0.1 --warmup_frac 0.05 --grad_clip 1.0

# r2d: Relative L2 loss (per-sample normalization, equalizes high/low Re)
./launch.sh 4 180 r2d_5L128h_relL2 --batch_size 4 --epochs 40 --loss_kind rel_l2 --warmup_frac 0.05 --grad_clip 1.0

# r2e: Per-channel pressure weighting (target metric is just pressure)
./launch.sh 6 180 r2e_5L128h_pw2 --batch_size 4 --epochs 40 --p_weight 2.0 --warmup_frac 0.05 --grad_clip 1.0

# r2f: Input noise as regularizer (MeshGraphNet style)
./launch.sh 7 180 r2f_5L128h_inoise005 --batch_size 4 --epochs 40 --input_noise_sigma 0.05 --warmup_frac 0.05 --grad_clip 1.0
}
sleep 5
nvidia-smi --query-gpu=memory.used --format=csv | head -10

arguments.content:
"""Ensemble evaluation: load multiple best checkpoints and average predictions on val/test.

Usage:
  python ensemble_eval.py \
    --checkpoint_paths model_dir1/checkpoint.pt,model_dir2/checkpoint.pt,... \
    --config_paths model_dir1/config.yaml,model_dir2/config.yaml,... \
    [--splits_dir /mnt/new-pvc/datasets/tandemfoil/splits_v2] \
    [--batch_size 4]

The script loads each model from its config + checkpoint, runs inference on the val
and test splits in normalized space, averages predictions across the ensemble, and
reports the same metrics as train.py for comparison.
"""

from __future__ import annotations
import argparse
import yaml
import torch
import torch.nn as nn
from pathlib import Path
from torch.utils.data import DataLoader

# Re-import from train.py
import sys
sys.path.insert(0, str(Path(__file__).resolve().parent))
# Note: import the model class from train.py without re-running its training script
import importlib.util
_train_path = Path(__file__).resolve().parent / "train.py"
_spec = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location("_train_module", _train_path)

# We'll import only the model class — but train.py runs at import time.
# So instead, recreate Transolver class here (verbatim from train.py).
import torch.nn.functional as F
from einops import rearrange
from timm.layers import trunc_normal_

ACTIVATION = {
    "gelu": nn.GELU,
    "tanh": nn.Tanh,
    "sigmoid": nn.Sigmoid,
    "relu": nn.ReLU,
    "leaky_relu": nn.LeakyReLU(0.1),
    "softplus": nn.Softplus,
    "ELU": nn.ELU,
    "silu": nn.SiLU,
}


class MLP(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, n_input, n_hidden, n_output, n_layers=1, act="gelu", res=True):
        super().__init__()
        act_fn = ACTIVATION[act]
        self.n_layers = n_layers
        self.res = res
        self.linear_pre = nn.Sequential(nn.Linear(n_input, n_hidden), act_fn())
        self.linear_post = nn.Linear(n_hidden, n_output)
        self.linears = nn.ModuleList(
            [nn.Sequential(nn.Linear(n_hidden, n_hidden), act_fn()) for _ in range(n_layers)]
        )

    def forward(self, x):
        x = self.linear_pre(x)
        for i in range(self.n_layers):
            x = self.linears[i](x) + x if self.res else self.linears[i](x)
        return self.linear_post(x)


class PhysicsAttention(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, dim, heads=8, dim_head=64, dropout=0.0, slice_num=64):
        super().__init__()
        inner_dim = dim_head * heads
        self.dim_head = dim_head
        self.heads = heads
        self.softmax = nn.Softmax(dim=-1)
        self.dropout = nn.Dropout(dropout)
        self.temperature = nn.Parameter(torch.ones([1, heads, 1, 1]) * 0.5)
        self.in_project_x = nn.Linear(dim, inner_dim)
        self.in_project_fx = nn.Linear(dim, inner_dim)
        self.in_project_slice = nn.Linear(dim_head, slice_num)
        torch.nn.init.orthogonal_(self.in_project_slice.weight)
        self.to_q = nn.Linear(dim_head, dim_head, bias=False)
        self.to_k = nn.Linear(dim_head, dim_head, bias=False)
        self.to_v = nn.Linear(dim_head, dim_head, bias=False)
        self.to_out = nn.Sequential(nn.Linear(inner_dim, dim), nn.Dropout(dropout))

    def forward(self, x):
        B, N, _ = x.shape
        fx_mid = (
            self.in_project_fx(x).reshape(B, N, self.heads, self.dim_head).permute(0, 2, 1, 3).contiguous()
        )
        x_mid = (
            self.in_project_x(x).reshape(B, N, self.heads, self.dim_head).permute(0, 2, 1, 3).contiguous()
        )
        slice_weights = self.softmax(self.in_project_slice(x_mid) / self.temperature)
        slice_norm = slice_weights.sum(2)
        slice_token = torch.einsum("bhnc,bhng->bhgc", fx_mid, slice_weights)
        slice_token = slice_token / ((slice_norm + 1e-5)[:, :, :, None].repeat(1, 1, 1, self.dim_head))
        q = self.to_q(slice_token)
        k = self.to_k(slice_token)
        v = self.to_v(slice_token)
        out_slice = F.scaled_dot_product_attention(
            q, k, v, dropout_p=self.dropout.p if self.training else 0.0, is_causal=False,
        )
        out_x = torch.einsum("bhgc,bhng->bhnc", out_slice, slice_weights)
        out_x = rearrange(out_x, "b h n d -> b n (h d)")
        return self.to_out(out_x)


class TransolverBlock(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, num_heads, hidden_dim, dropout, act="gelu",
                 mlp_ratio=4, last_layer=False, out_dim=1, slice_num=32):
        super().__init__()
        self.last_layer = last_layer
        self.ln_1 = nn.LayerNorm(hidden_dim)
        self.attn = PhysicsAttention(
            hidden_dim, heads=num_heads, dim_head=hidden_dim // num_heads,
            dropout=dropout, slice_num=slice_num,
        )
        self.ln_2 = nn.LayerNorm(hidden_dim)
        self.mlp = MLP(hidden_dim, hidden_dim * mlp_ratio, hidden_dim,
                       n_layers=0, res=False, act=act)
        if self.last_layer:
            self.ln_3 = nn.LayerNorm(hidden_dim)
            self.mlp2 = nn.Sequential(
                nn.Linear(hidden_dim, hidden_dim), nn.GELU(),
                nn.Linear(hidden_dim, out_dim),
            )

    def forward(self, fx):
        fx = self.attn(self.ln_1(fx)) + fx
        fx = self.mlp(self.ln_2(fx)) + fx
        if self.last_layer:
            return self.mlp2(self.ln_3(fx))
        return fx


class Transolver(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, space_dim=1, n_layers=5, n_hidden=256, dropout=0.0,
                 n_head=8, act="gelu", mlp_ratio=1, fun_dim=1, out_dim=1,
                 slice_num=32, ref=8, unified_pos=False,
                 slice_nums=None,
                 output_fields=None, output_dims=None):
        super().__init__()
        self.ref = ref
        self.unified_pos = unified_pos
        self.output_fields = output_fields or []
        self.output_dims = output_dims or []
        if self.unified_pos:
            self.preprocess = MLP(fun_dim + ref**3, n_hidden * 2, n_hidden,
                                  n_layers=0, res=False, act=act)
        else:
            self.preprocess = MLP(fun_dim + space_dim, n_hidden * 2, n_hidden,
                                  n_layers=0, res=False, act=act)
        self.n_hidden = n_hidden
        self.space_dim = space_dim
        if slice_nums is None:
            slice_nums = [slice_num] * n_layers
        self.blocks = nn.ModuleList([
            TransolverBlock(
                num_heads=n_head, hidden_dim=n_hidden, dropout=dropout,
                act=act, mlp_ratio=mlp_ratio, out_dim=out_dim,
                slice_num=slice_nums[i], last_layer=(i == n_layers - 1),
            )
            for i in range(n_layers)
        ])
        self.placeholder = nn.Parameter((1 / n_hidden) * torch.rand(n_hidden))
        self.apply(self._init_weights)

    def _init_weights(self, m):
        if isinstance(m, nn.Linear):
            trunc_normal_(m.weight, std=0.02)
            if m.bias is not None:
                nn.init.constant_(m.bias, 0)
        elif isinstance(m, (nn.LayerNorm, nn.BatchNorm1d)):
            nn.init.constant_(m.bias, 0)
            nn.init.constant_(m.weight, 1.0)

    def forward(self, data, **kwargs):
        x = data["x"]
        fx = self.preprocess(x) + self.placeholder[None, None, :]
        for block in self.blocks:
            fx = block(fx)
        return {"preds": fx}


def main():
    from data import (
        VAL_SPLIT_NAMES,
        TEST_SPLIT_NAMES,
        load_data,
        load_test_data,
        pad_collate,
        accumulate_batch,
        finalize_split,
        aggregate_splits,
    )

    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser()
    parser.add_argument("--checkpoint_paths", required=True, type=str,
                        help="Comma-separated checkpoint paths")
    parser.add_argument("--config_paths", required=True, type=str,
                        help="Comma-separated config paths")
    parser.add_argument("--splits_dir", default="/mnt/new-pvc/datasets/tandemfoil/splits_v2")
    parser.add_argument("--batch_size", default=4, type=int)
    parser.add_argument("--skip_test", action="store_true")
    args = parser.parse_args()

    ckpts = args.checkpoint_paths.split(",")
    cfgs = args.config_paths.split(",")
    assert len(ckpts) == len(cfgs), "checkpoint_paths and config_paths must have same length"

    device = torch.device("cuda" if torch.cuda.is_available() else "cpu")
    print(f"Device: {device}, ensemble size: {len(ckpts)}")

    # Load data
    train_ds, val_splits, stats, _ = load_data(args.splits_dir, debug=False)
    stats = {k: v.to(device) for k, v in stats.items()}
    loader_kwargs = dict(collate_fn=pad_collate, num_workers=4, pin_memory=True,
                         persistent_workers=True, prefetch_factor=2)
    val_loaders = {
        name: DataLoader(ds, batch_size=args.batch_size, shuffle=False, **loader_kwargs)
        for name, ds in val_splits.items()
    }

    # Load all models
    models = []
    for ckpt_path, cfg_path in zip(ckpts, cfgs):
        with open(cfg_path) as f:
            mc = yaml.safe_load(f)
        # Convert known fields to matching names if needed
        m = Transolver(**mc).to(device)
        sd = torch.load(ckpt_path, map_location=device, weights_only=True)
        m.load_state_dict(sd)
        m.eval()
        models.append(m)
        print(f"  Loaded {Path(ckpt_path).parent.name}: {sum(p.numel() for p in m.parameters())/1e6:.2f}M params")

    def evaluate_split_ensemble(loader):
        mae_surf = torch.zeros(3, dtype=torch.float64, device=device)
        mae_vol = torch.zeros(3, dtype=torch.float64, device=device)
        n_surf = n_vol = 0
        with torch.no_grad():
            for x, y, is_surface, mask in loader:
                x = x.to(device, non_blocking=True)
                y = y.to(device, non_blocking=True)
                is_surface = is_surface.to(device, non_blocking=True)
                mask = mask.to(device, non_blocking=True)
                x_norm = (x - stats["x_mean"]) / stats["x_std"]
                # Average predictions across models in normalized space
                pred_sum = None
                for m in models:
                    p = m({"x": x_norm})["preds"]
                    pred_sum = p if pred_sum is None else pred_sum + p
                pred = pred_sum / len(models)
                pred_orig = pred * stats["y_std"] + stats["y_mean"]
                ds, dv = accumulate_batch(pred_orig, y, is_surface, mask, mae_surf, mae_vol)
                n_surf += ds
                n_vol += dv
        return finalize_split(mae_surf, mae_vol, n_surf, n_vol)

    print("\n=== Validation ensemble ===")
    val_metrics = {name: evaluate_split_ensemble(loader) for name, loader in val_loaders.items()}
    val_avg = aggregate_splits(val_metrics)
    print(f"  val_avg/mae_surf_p = {val_avg['avg/mae_surf_p']:.4f}")
    for name, m in val_metrics.items():
        print(f"  {name:30s} surf_p={m['mae_surf_p']:.2f} vol_p={m['mae_vol_p']:.2f} "
              f"surf_Ux={m['mae_surf_Ux']:.2f} surf_Uy={m['mae_surf_Uy']:.2f}")

    if not args.skip_test:
        print("\n=== Test ensemble ===")
        test_datasets = load_test_data(args.splits_dir, debug=False)
        test_loaders = {
            name: DataLoader(ds, batch_size=args.batch_size, shuffle=False, **loader_kwargs)
            for name, ds in test_datasets.items()
        }
        test_metrics = {name: evaluate_split_ensemble(loader) for name, loader in test_loaders.items()}
        test_avg = aggregate_splits(test_metrics)
        print(f"  test_avg/mae_surf_p = {test_avg['avg/mae_surf_p']:.4f}")
        for name, m in test_metrics.items():
            print(f"  {name:30s} surf_p={m['mae_surf_p']:.2f} vol_p={m['mae_vol_p']:.2f}")
        return val_avg, test_avg
    return val_avg, None


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

cd /workspace/runs && {
# Round 3 NEW: 5 ensemble candidates - mostly same recipe as r2d (which got 56.7), different seeds
# Plus 1 amp/bs8 variant for backup

# r3d: same as r2d but seed=1, longer time (240min, --epochs 50)
./launch.sh 1 240 r3d_relL2_seed1 --batch_size 4 --epochs 50 --loss_kind rel_l2 --warmup_frac 0.05 --grad_clip 1.0 --seed 1

# r3e: same as r2d, seed=2
./launch.sh 3 240 r3e_relL2_seed2 --batch_size 4 --epochs 50 --loss_kind rel_l2 --warmup_frac 0.05 --grad_clip 1.0 --seed 2

# r3f: same as r2d, seed=3
./launch.sh 4 240 r3f_relL2_seed3 --batch_size 4 --epochs 50 --loss_kind rel_l2 --warmup_frac 0.05 --grad_clip 1.0 --seed 3

# r3g: amp + larger batch (bf16 mixed precision, bs=8 instead of 4)
./launch.sh 6 240 r3g_relL2_amp_bs8 --batch_size 8 --epochs 50 --loss_kind rel_l2 --warmup_frac 0.05 --grad_clip 1.0 --amp

# r3h: r2d + longer warmup (10%)
./launch.sh 7 240 r3h_relL2_warm10 --batch_size 4 --epochs 50 --loss_kind rel_l2 --warmup_frac 0.10 --grad_clip 1.0
}
sleep 8
nvidia-smi --query-gpu=index,memory.used --format=csv | head -10

arguments.command:
cd /workspace/ml-intern-benchmark/target && python3 << 'EOF' 2>&1 | tail -30
import wandb
api = wandb.Api()
runs = api.runs('wandb-applied-ai-team/senpai-v1-ml-intern', filters={'group': 'mlintern-pai2-24h-v3-r3'})
results = []
for r in runs:
    name = r.name.replace('mlintern-pai2-24h-v3-r3/','')
    try:
        h = list(r.scan_history(keys=['val_avg/mae_surf_p']))
        vals = [(i, x['val_avg/mae_surf_p']) for i,x in enumerate(h) if 'val_avg/mae_surf_p' in x]
        if not vals: continue
        best_idx, best_v = min(vals, key=lambda v: v[1])
        latest_v = vals[-1][1]
        results.append((name, r.state, best_v, best_idx+1, latest_v, len(vals)))
    except: pass

results.sort(key=lambda r: r[2])
print(f'name                                       | state    | BEST  @ ep | latest | epochs')
for name, state, best, ep, last, n in results:
    print(f'{name:42s} | {state:8s} | {best:5.1f} @ {ep:2d} | {last:6.1f} | {n}')
EOF

cd /workspace/ml-intern-benchmark/target && python3 << 'EOF' 2>&1 | tail -30
import wandb
api = wandb.Api()
runs = api.runs('wandb-applied-ai-team/senpai-v1-ml-intern', filters={'group': 'mlintern-pai2-24h-v3-r3'})
for r in runs:
    name = r.name.replace('mlintern-pai2-24h-v3-r3/','')
    if name == 'r2d_5L128h_relL2':
        s = r.summary
        print(f'=== r2d_5L128h_relL2 ===')

        print(f'total_train_minutes: {s.get("total_train_minutes")}')
        for k in ['test/test_single_in_dist/mae_surf_p',
                  'test/test_geom_camber_rc/mae_surf_p',
                  'test/test_geom_camber_cruise/mae_surf_p',

                  'test_avg/mae_surf_p',

        # Also check artifact

EOF

with torch.no_grad():
        for x, y, is_surface, mask in loader:
            x = x.to(device, non_blocking=True)
            y = y.to(device, non_blocking=True)
            is_surface = is_surface.to(device, non_blocking=True)
            mask = mask.to(device, non_blocking=True)

            # Pre-filter non-finite samples (test_geom_camber_cruise sample 000020 has Inf in y).
            # Without this, NaN propagates: (pred - inf).abs() = inf; inf * 0.0 = NaN, contaminating
            # the masked-sum even when scoring.py's sample_mask is False for that sample.
            # Safe replacement: zero out non-finite y BEFORE the err compute. scoring.py's
            # per-sample finite check still excludes the sample from the metric (sample_mask=0),
            # but err is now finite so masked sum is clean.
            y_finite_per_sample = torch.isfinite(y.reshape(y.shape[0], -1)).all(dim=-1)  # [B]
            y_clean = torch.where(
                y_finite_per_sample.view(-1, 1, 1).expand_as(y),
                y,
                torch.zeros_like(y),
            )

            x_norm = (x - stats["x_mean"]) / stats["x_std"]
            y_norm = (y_clean - stats["y_mean"]) / stats["y_std"]
            pred = model({"x": x_norm})["preds"]

            sq_err = (pred - y_norm) ** 2
            # Apply finite-sample mask to vol/surf masks for the normalized loss
            sample_mask_2d = y_finite_per_sample.view(-1, 1).expand_as(mask)
            vol_mask = mask & ~is_surface & sample_mask_2d
            surf_mask = mask & is_surface & sample_mask_2d
            vol_loss_sum += (
                (sq_err * vol_mask.unsqueeze(-1)).sum()
                / vol_mask.sum().clamp(min=1)
            ).item()
            surf_loss_sum += (
                (sq_err * surf_mask.unsqueeze(-1)).sum()
                / surf_mask.sum().clamp(min=1)
            ).item()
            n_batches += 1

            pred_orig = pred * stats["y_std"] + stats["y_mean"]
            # Pass y_clean to scoring; scoring.py still applies its sample_mask filter
            # so the bad sample is excluded from MAE accumulators.
            ds, dv = accumulate_batch(pred_orig, y_clean, is_surface, mask, mae_surf, mae_vol)
            n_surf += ds
            n_vol += dv

arguments.new_str:
with torch.no_grad():
        for x, y, is_surface, mask in loader:
            x = x.to(device, non_blocking=True)
            y = y.to(device, non_blocking=True)
            is_surface = is_surface.to(device, non_blocking=True)
            mask = mask.to(device, non_blocking=True)

            # Some test samples have non-finite (Inf) values in y (e.g. test_geom_camber_cruise
            # sample 000020). data/scoring.py is supposed to skip these via its sample_mask,
            # but the err = (pred - inf).abs() = inf computed BEFORE masking propagates NaN
            # via inf * 0.0 = NaN inside the masked sum. We pre-filter the batch to only
            # contain finite-y samples; scoring.py then runs over a clean batch and produces
            # clean accumulators. The skipped sample is correctly excluded from the metric.
            y_finite_per_sample = torch.isfinite(y.reshape(y.shape[0], -1)).all(dim=-1)  # [B]
            x_norm = (x - stats["x_mean"]) / stats["x_std"]
            y_norm = (y - stats["y_mean"]) / stats["y_std"]
            pred = model({"x": x_norm})["preds"]

            # Loss / MAE computations restricted to finite-y samples
            if y_finite_per_sample.all():
                # fast path
                sq_err = (pred - y_norm) ** 2
                vol_mask = mask & ~is_surface
                surf_mask = mask & is_surface
                vol_loss_sum += (
                    (sq_err * vol_mask.unsqueeze(-1)).sum()
                    / vol_mask.sum().clamp(min=1)
                ).item()
                surf_loss_sum += (
                    (sq_err * surf_mask.unsqueeze(-1)).sum()
                    / surf_mask.sum().clamp(min=1)
                ).item()
                n_batches += 1

                pred_orig = pred * stats["y_std"] + stats["y_mean"]
                ds, dv = accumulate_batch(pred_orig, y, is_surface, mask, mae_surf, mae_vol)
                n_surf += ds
                n_vol += dv
            elif y_finite_per_sample.any():
                # filter batch to good samples only — y_norm rows of bad samples may contain
                # +/-inf, so we slice them out before any err computation
                good_idx = torch.where(y_finite_per_sample)[0]
                pred_g = pred[good_idx]
                y_norm_g = y_norm[good_idx]
                y_g = y[good_idx]
                is_surf_g = is_surface[good_idx]
                mask_g = mask[good_idx]
                sq_err = (pred_g - y_norm_g) ** 2
                vol_mask = mask_g & ~is_surf_g
                surf_mask = mask_g & is_surf_g
                vol_loss_sum += (
                    (sq_err * vol_mask.unsqueeze(-1)).sum()
                    / vol_mask.sum().clamp(min=1)
                ).item()
                surf_loss_sum += (
                    (sq_err * surf_mask.unsqueeze(-1)).sum()
                    / surf_mask.sum().clamp(min=1)
                ).item()
                n_batches += 1

                pred_g_orig = pred_g * stats["y_std"] + stats["y_mean"]
                ds, dv = accumulate_batch(pred_g_orig, y_g, is_surf_g, mask_g, mae_surf, mae_vol)
                n_surf += ds
                n_vol += dv
            # else: all samples in this batch are bad — skip entirely

import wandb
api = wandb.Api()
runs = api.runs('wandb-applied-ai-team/senpai-v1-ml-intern', filters={'group': 'mlintern-pai2-24h-v3-r3'})
results = []
for r in runs:
    name = r.name.replace('mlintern-pai2-24h-v3-r3/','')
    try:
        h = list(r.scan_history(keys=['val_avg/mae_surf_p']))
        vals = [(i, x['val_avg/mae_surf_p']) for i,x in enumerate(h) if 'val_avg/mae_surf_p' in x]
        if not vals: continue
        best_idx, best_v = min(vals, key=lambda v: v[1])
        latest_v = vals[-1][1]
        results.append((name, r.state, best_v, best_idx+1, latest_v, len(vals)))
    except: pass
results.sort(key=lambda r: r[2])
for name, state, best, ep, last, n in results[:15]:
    print(f'{name:42s} | {state:8s} | {best:5.1f} @ {ep:2d} | {last:6.1f} | {n}')

arguments.new_str:
def evaluate_split_ensemble(loader):
        mae_surf = torch.zeros(3, dtype=torch.float64, device=device)
        mae_vol = torch.zeros(3, dtype=torch.float64, device=device)
        n_surf = n_vol = 0
        with torch.no_grad():
            for x, y, is_surface, mask in loader:
                x = x.to(device, non_blocking=True)
                y = y.to(device, non_blocking=True)
                is_surface = is_surface.to(device, non_blocking=True)
                mask = mask.to(device, non_blocking=True)

                # Filter non-finite-y samples (e.g. test_geom_camber_cruise sample 000020)
                # before passing to scoring; scoring.py's masked-sum has a NaN propagation
                # bug for inf*0=NaN, so we keep the bad sample out of the batch entirely.
                y_finite = torch.isfinite(y.reshape(y.shape[0], -1)).all(dim=-1)
                if not y_finite.any():
                    continue
                if not y_finite.all():
                    good = torch.where(y_finite)[0]
                    x = x[good]
                    y = y[good]
                    is_surface = is_surface[good]
                    mask = mask[good]

                x_norm = (x - stats["x_mean"]) / stats["x_std"]
                # Average predictions across models in normalized space
                pred_sum = None
                for m in models:
                    p = m({"x": x_norm})["preds"]
                    pred_sum = p if pred_sum is None else pred_sum + p
                pred = pred_sum / len(models)
                pred_orig = pred * stats["y_std"] + stats["y_mean"]
                ds, dv = accumulate_batch(pred_orig, y, is_surface, mask, mae_surf, mae_vol)
                n_surf += ds
                n_vol += dv
        return finalize_split(mae_surf, mae_vol, n_surf, n_vol)
arguments.old_str:
def evaluate_split_ensemble(loader):
        mae_surf = torch.zeros(3, dtype=torch.float64, device=device)
        mae_vol = torch.zeros(3, dtype=torch.float64, device=device)
        n_surf = n_vol = 0
        with torch.no_grad():
            for x, y, is_surface, mask in loader:
                x = x.to(device, non_blocking=True)
                y = y.to(device, non_blocking=True)
                is_surface = is_surface.to(device, non_blocking=True)
                mask = mask.to(device, non_blocking=True)
                x_norm = (x - stats["x_mean"]) / stats["x_std"]
                # Average predictions across models in normalized space
                pred_sum = None
                for m in models:
                    p = m({"x": x_norm})["preds"]
                    pred_sum = p if pred_sum is None else pred_sum + p
                pred = pred_sum / len(models)
                pred_orig = pred * stats["y_std"] + stats["y_mean"]
                ds, dv = accumulate_batch(pred_orig, y, is_surface, mask, mae_surf, mae_vol)
                n_surf += ds
                n_vol += dv
        return finalize_split(mae_surf, mae_vol, n_surf, n_vol)

cd /workspace/ml-intern-benchmark/target && git add -A train.py ensemble_eval.py research/MLINTERN_RESULTS.jsonl && git commit -m "Round 1-3 experiments: relL2 loss + warmup achieves val_avg/mae_surf_p 56.7

Summary of changes:
- train.py: Add CLI flags for architecture (n_hidden/n_layers/n_head/slice_num/mlp_ratio/dropout/multiscale_slice),
  optimizer (beta2, grad_clip, warmup_frac, lr_schedule), loss (loss_kind ∈ {mse,l1,rel_l2}, p_weight,
  Ux_weight, Uy_weight, input_noise_sigma), and mixed precision (amp).
- train.py: Fix NaN propagation bug in evaluate_split when y has Inf values (test_geom_camber_cruise
  sample 000020). Pre-filter non-finite samples from each batch before computing err so masked sums
  stay finite. scoring.py and the metric definition are unchanged; the bad sample is correctly
  excluded from the metric (matches the documented 'per-sample skip for non-finite ground truth').
- ensemble_eval.py: New script for averaging predictions across multiple checkpoints. Includes the
  same NaN pre-filter so test_geom_camber_cruise is scored correctly.

Round 1 finding: 8L/256H/8head/sn32 (AirfRANS-style upgrade) overfit fast on small dataset (1499 train);
baseline 5L/128H/4head/sn64 with --epochs 999 (flat LR) reached val_avg/mae_surf_p ~103.

Round 2 finding: with --epochs 40 (proper cosine decay) and warmup, even baseline arch hits ~62.
Switching MSE → relative-L2 loss (per-sample normalized) gives the largest single jump:
- r2d (relL2 + warmup + grad_clip + cosine, --epochs 40): val_avg/mae_surf_p = 56.72,
  test_avg/mae_surf_p = 49.96 (single model, after NaN fix)

Round 3 (in progress): multi-seed replicas + dropout + amp variants for ensemble." 2>&1 | tail -10

runs = api.runs('wandb-applied-ai-team/senpai-v1-ml-intern', filters={'group': 'mlintern-pai2-24h-v3-r3'})
# Find finished models with best_val_avg/mae_surf_p < 100
best_runs = []
for r in runs:
    name = r.name.replace('mlintern-pai2-24h-v3-r3/','')
    s = r.summary
    best_p = s.get('best_val_avg/mae_surf_p')
    if r.state == 'finished' and isinstance(best_p, float) and best_p < 100:
        # Find the local model dir
        d = Path(f'models/model-{r.id}')
        if d.exists() and (d / 'checkpoint.pt').exists():
            best_runs.append((name, best_p, str(d / 'checkpoint.pt'), str(d / 'config.yaml')))

best_runs.sort(key=lambda r: r[1])
print(f'Found {len(best_runs)} finished+saved models with val < 100')
for name, p, ck, cf in best_runs:
    print(f'  {name:42s} val={p:.2f}')

# Save for ensemble
ckpts = ','.join(r[2] for r in best_runs)
cfgs = ','.join(r[3] for r in best_runs)
with open('/tmp/ensemble_paths.txt', 'w') as f:
    f.write(f'CHECKPOINTS={ckpts}\n')
    f.write(f'CONFIGS={cfgs}\n')
print(f'Saved {len(best_runs)} paths to /tmp/ensemble_paths.txt')
EOF

arguments.command:
sleep 1200
date +%H:%M:%S

cd /workspace/ml-intern-benchmark/target && python3 << 'EOF' 2>&1 | tail -25
import wandb
api = wandb.Api()
runs = api.runs('wandb-applied-ai-team/senpai-v1-ml-intern', filters={'group': 'mlintern-pai2-24h-v3-r3'})
results = []
for r in runs:
    name = r.name.replace('mlintern-pai2-24h-v3-r3/','')
    try:
        h = list(r.scan_history(keys=['val_avg/mae_surf_p']))
        vals = [(i, x['val_avg/mae_surf_p']) for i,x in enumerate(h) if 'val_avg/mae_surf_p' in x]
        if not vals: continue
        best_idx, best_v = min(vals, key=lambda v: v[1])
        latest_v = vals[-1][1]
        results.append((name, r.state, best_v, best_idx+1, latest_v, len(vals)))
    except: pass
results.sort(key=lambda r: r[2])
for name, state, best, ep, last, n in results[:20]:
    print(f'{name:42s} | {state:8s} | {best:5.1f} @ {ep:2d} | {last:6.1f} | {n}')
EOF

# 6 GPUs free. Launch round 4 batch
cd /workspace/runs && {
# Round 4 main batch: r3a-style with diverse seeds + variations, --epochs 80, 6h timeout
# Goal: ensemble of 8 strong models

# r4c: seed=6, drop=0.1 (r3a clone)
./launch.sh 1 360 r4c_relL2_drop01_s6 --batch_size 4 --epochs 80 --loss_kind rel_l2 --dropout 0.1 --warmup_frac 0.05 --grad_clip 1.0 --seed 6

# r4d: seed=7, drop=0.1
./launch.sh 3 360 r4d_relL2_drop01_s7 --batch_size 4 --epochs 80 --loss_kind rel_l2 --dropout 0.1 --warmup_frac 0.05 --grad_clip 1.0 --seed 7

# r4e: seed=8, drop=0.0 (no dropout - test if drop helps)
./launch.sh 4 360 r4e_relL2_drop0_s8 --batch_size 4 --epochs 80 --loss_kind rel_l2 --dropout 0.0 --warmup_frac 0.05 --grad_clip 1.0 --seed 8

# r4f: drop=0.15, seed=9 (heavier dropout)
./launch.sh 5 360 r4f_relL2_drop015_s9 --batch_size 4 --epochs 80 --loss_kind rel_l2 --dropout 0.15 --warmup_frac 0.05 --grad_clip 1.0 --seed 9

# r4g: r3a style + input noise=0.02 + seed=10
./launch.sh 6 360 r4g_relL2_drop01_inoise_s10 --batch_size 4 --epochs 80 --loss_kind rel_l2 --dropout 0.1 --warmup_frac 0.05 --grad_clip 1.0 --input_noise_sigma 0.02 --seed 10

arguments.command:
# Run 5-model ensemble (now that r3a is done) - use a free GPU
# All round 4 are running on GPUs 0-7, but we need GPU for ensemble eval
# Looking at memory: GPU 4 has 38275 MiB - lower. Let me use a batch with smaller mem footprint
nvidia-smi --query-gpu=index,memory.used --format=csv | head -10
echo "---"
# Find best 5 finished checkpoints
cd /workspace/ml-intern-benchmark/target && python3 << 'EOF' 2>&1 | tail -10
import wandb
from pathlib import Path
api = wandb.Api()
runs = api.runs('wandb-applied-ai-team/senpai-v1-ml-intern', filters={'group': 'mlintern-pai2-24h-v3-r3'})
top5 = []
for r in runs:
    name = r.name.replace('mlintern-pai2-24h-v3-r3/','')
    s = r.summary
    best_p = s.get('best_val_avg/mae_surf_p')
    if r.state == 'finished' and isinstance(best_p, float) and best_p < 70:
        d = Path(f'models/model-{r.id}')
        if d.exists() and (d / 'checkpoint.pt').exists():
            top5.append((best_p, name, str(d / 'checkpoint.pt'), str(d / 'config.yaml')))

top5.sort()
print('Top 5+:')
for p, name, ck, cf in top5[:8]:
    print(f'  {name:42s} val={p:.2f} → {ck}')
EOF

arguments.command:
date +%H:%M:%S
cd /workspace/ml-intern-benchmark/target && python3 << 'EOF' 2>&1 | tail -25
import wandb
api = wandb.Api()
runs = api.runs('wandb-applied-ai-team/senpai-v1-ml-intern', filters={'group': 'mlintern-pai2-24h-v3-r3'})
results = []
for r in runs:
    name = r.name.replace('mlintern-pai2-24h-v3-r3/','')
    try:
        h = list(r.scan_history(keys=['val_avg/mae_surf_p']))
        vals = [(i, x['val_avg/mae_surf_p']) for i,x in enumerate(h) if 'val_avg/mae_surf_p' in x]
        if not vals: continue
        best_idx, best_v = min(vals, key=lambda v: v[1])
        latest_v = vals[-1][1]
        results.append((name, r.state, best_v, best_idx+1, latest_v, len(vals)))
    except: pass
results.sort(key=lambda r: r[2])
for name, state, best, ep, last, n in results[:15]:
    print(f'{name:42s} | {state:8s} | {best:5.1f} @ {ep:2d} | {last:6.1f} | {n}')
EOF

        results.append((name, r.state, best_v, best_idx+1, latest_v, len(vals)))
    except: pass
results.sort(key=lambda r: r[2])
for name, state, best, ep, last, n in results[:18]:
    print(f'{name:42s} | {state:8s} | {best:5.1f} @ {ep:2d} | {last:6.1f} | {n}')
EOF

data.content: Let me also implement model soup (weight averaging) to compare with prediction ensemble:

arguments.content:
"""Model soup: average weights of multiple checkpoints (vs ensemble of predictions).

Loads multiple model checkpoints, averages their state dicts, evaluates the
single resulting model on val/test splits. Models must have the same architecture.

Usage:
  python model_soup.py \
    --checkpoint_paths model_dir1/checkpoint.pt,model_dir2/checkpoint.pt,... \
    --config_paths model_dir1/config.yaml,model_dir2/config.yaml,... \
    [--splits_dir /mnt/new-pvc/datasets/tandemfoil/splits_v2] \
    [--batch_size 4]
"""

from __future__ import annotations
import argparse
import yaml
import torch
from pathlib import Path
from torch.utils.data import DataLoader

# Reuse model definition from ensemble_eval.py
import importlib.util
_path = Path(__file__).resolve().parent / "ensemble_eval.py"
_spec = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location("_ens_module", _path)
_mod = importlib.util.module_from_spec(_spec)
_spec.loader.exec_module(_mod)
Transolver = _mod.Transolver


def main():
    from data import (
        VAL_SPLIT_NAMES,
        TEST_SPLIT_NAMES,
        load_data,
        load_test_data,
        pad_collate,
        accumulate_batch,
        finalize_split,
        aggregate_splits,
    )

    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser()
    parser.add_argument("--checkpoint_paths", required=True, type=str)
    parser.add_argument("--config_paths", required=True, type=str)
    parser.add_argument("--splits_dir", default="/mnt/new-pvc/datasets/tandemfoil/splits_v2")
    parser.add_argument("--batch_size", default=4, type=int)
    parser.add_argument("--skip_test", action="store_true")
    args = parser.parse_args()

    ckpts = args.checkpoint_paths.split(",")
    cfgs = args.config_paths.split(",")
    assert len(ckpts) == len(cfgs)

    device = torch.device("cuda" if torch.cuda.is_available() else "cpu")
    print(f"Device: {device}, soup size: {len(ckpts)}")

    # Load first model as template
    with open(cfgs[0]) as f:
        mc = yaml.safe_load(f)
    model = Transolver(**mc).to(device)

    # Load all state dicts and average them
    avg_sd = None
    for ckpt_path in ckpts:
        sd = torch.load(ckpt_path, map_location=device, weights_only=True)
        if avg_sd is None:
            avg_sd = {k: v.float().clone() for k, v in sd.items()}
        else:
            for k in avg_sd:
                avg_sd[k] += sd[k].float()
    for k in avg_sd:
        avg_sd[k] /= len(ckpts)

    model.load_state_dict(avg_sd)
    model.eval()
    print(f"  Loaded {sum(p.numel() for p in model.parameters())/1e6:.2f}M param soup")

    # Load data
    train_ds, val_splits, stats, _ = load_data(args.splits_dir, debug=False)
    stats = {k: v.to(device) for k, v in stats.items()}
    loader_kwargs = dict(collate_fn=pad_collate, num_workers=4, pin_memory=True,
                         persistent_workers=True, prefetch_factor=2)
    val_loaders = {
        name: DataLoader(ds, batch_size=args.batch_size, shuffle=False, **loader_kwargs)
        for name, ds in val_splits.items()
    }

    def evaluate_split_one(loader):
        mae_surf = torch.zeros(3, dtype=torch.float64, device=device)
        mae_vol = torch.zeros(3, dtype=torch.float64, device=device)
        n_surf = n_vol = 0
        with torch.no_grad():
            for x, y, is_surface, mask in loader:
                x = x.to(device, non_blocking=True)
                y = y.to(device, non_blocking=True)
                is_surface = is_surface.to(device, non_blocking=True)
                mask = mask.to(device, non_blocking=True)
                y_finite = torch.isfinite(y.reshape(y.shape[0], -1)).all(dim=-1)
                if not y_finite.any():
                    continue
                if not y_finite.all():
                    good = torch.where(y_finite)[0]
                    x = x[good]; y = y[good]; is_surface = is_surface[good]; mask = mask[good]
                x_norm = (x - stats["x_mean"]) / stats["x_std"]
                pred = model({"x": x_norm})["preds"]
                pred_orig = pred * stats["y_std"] + stats["y_mean"]
                ds, dv = accumulate_batch(pred_orig, y, is_surface, mask, mae_surf, mae_vol)
                n_surf += ds; n_vol += dv
        return finalize_split(mae_surf, mae_vol, n_surf, n_vol)

    print("\n=== Validation soup ===")
    val_metrics = {name: evaluate_split_one(loader) for name, loader in val_loaders.items()}
    val_avg = aggregate_splits(val_metrics)
    print(f"  val_avg/mae_surf_p = {val_avg['avg/mae_surf_p']:.4f}")
    for name, m in val_metrics.items():
        print(f"  {name:30s} surf_p={m['mae_surf_p']:.2f} vol_p={m['mae_vol_p']:.2f}")

    if not args.skip_test:
        print("\n=== Test soup ===")
        test_datasets = load_test_data(args.splits_dir, debug=False)
        test_loaders = {
            name: DataLoader(ds, batch_size=args.batch_size, shuffle=False, **loader_kwargs)
            for name, ds in test_datasets.items()
        }
        test_metrics = {name: evaluate_split_one(loader) for name, loader in test_loaders.items()}
        test_avg = aggregate_splits(test_metrics)
        print(f"  test_avg/mae_surf_p = {test_avg['avg/mae_surf_p']:.4f}")
        for name, m in test_metrics.items():
            print(f"  {name:30s} surf_p={m['mae_surf_p']:.2f} vol_p={m['mae_vol_p']:.2f}")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
